title: Ensure Strings Literals are on the Left When Checking for Equality
description: String literals should be placed on the left-hand side of an equals() or equalsIgnoreCase() method call to prevent null pointer exceptions. As a string literal can never be null by definition, this practice helps in dealing with the null cases appropriately.
recommendation: Place the string literal on the left-hand side of the equals() or equalsIgnoreCase() method call. For instance, instead of 'myString.equals("foo")', use '"foo".equals(myString)'.
impact: Prevents potential null pointer exceptions which can cause program to crash, improving the robustness of the code.

checks.message: Place the string literal on the left-hand side of the equals() or equalsIgnoreCase() method call. For instance, instead of 'myString.equals("foo")', use '"foo".equals(myString)'.
